validated_commands.quick_start: uv sync --frozen && uv run pytest -m "not slow" && uv run mypy
notes: uv installable via curl; needs PATH update after install
uv sync --frozen installs exact pinned deps from uv.lock
benchmark fixture comes from pytest-codspeed; DO NOT use -p no:codspeed (breaks benchmark tests)
slow marker deselects long-running tests; benchmark tests are NOT marked slow
uv run pytest -m 'not slow' = 187 passed (benchmark tests included, pass via codspeed fixture)

open_prs.title: perf: use np.einsum for sum-of-squares to avoid temporary array allocation
open_prs.note: 16-26% improvement. integrated -26%, momentary -17%, short_term -18%, lra -18%. 946 tests pass, mypy clean.

closed_prs.outcome: MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-05-03. Fused square-sum to avoid redundant quarter-block read. ~2% improvement on momentary/short_term/lra.
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-05-02. NOTE: only added TruePeakProcessor benchmark in tests/test_processing.py; the RunningSum->float change was NOT in the commit. Memory was wrong.
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-04-30. Incremental running sums for momentary/short_term: momentary -6.2%, short_term -4.7%, lra -5.7%.
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-04-29. np.dot for channel-weighted sums.
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-04-28. CodSpeed: +7% on momentary/short_term/lra individual benchmarks, +2% overall.
CLOSED without merge. CodSpeed +3% individual, 1% overall.
MERGED. Algorithmic O(n^2 log n) -> O(n log n) change showed 37% improvement in CodSpeed.

optimization_backlog.desc: Use np.einsum('ij,ij->i', x, x) instead of np.square(x).sum(axis=1) in process_block(). Avoids allocating intermediate (num_channels, N) array. 16-26% improvement locally. PR submitted 2026-05-04.
Replacing _momentary_sum/_short_term_sum RunningSum with plain floats. Retested 2026-05-02: before ~3.16ms, after ~3.19ms. NO measurable improvement. Do not retry.
optimization_backlog.status: TESTED - NO IMPROVEMENT. Do not attempt again.

optimization_backlog.desc: lk_log10() uses np.log10 for scalars; math.log10 is ~1.4x faster for scalar path. ~1% benchmark improvement only.
optimization_backlog.impact: ~1% per benchmark mode (too small for CodSpeed, probably)

optimization_backlog.desc: Maintainer is open to exploring vendoring/trimming _upfirdn_apply.pyx on a SEPARATE branch (not main). Requires Cython. High complexity but potentially significant impact (~55% CPU).
optimization_backlog.impact: Up to 55% of Meter.write time is TruePeak FIR
optimization_backlog.feasibility: low-medium (complex, maintainer interested in separate branch exploration)

optimization_backlog.desc: _calc_gating does O(block_index) numpy work per block when rel_threshold changes; O(n^2) total.

maintainer_instructions: Remove reset() optimization from backlog - moved to teardown in benchmarks (PR #99, 2026-04-29)
Open to exploring _upfirdn_apply.pyx vendoring on separate branch (not main) - mentioned 2026-04-30 in issue #71 comment
2026-05-02: 'Re-run task 1 (discover and validate commands). Check AGENTS.md while doing so' - DONE 2026-05-02
perf_notes: CodSpeed 'does not alter performance' = overall improvement below 10% threshold. Individual benchmarks accessible via CodSpeed link.
Only algorithmic changes or large constant-factor changes exceed 10% CodSpeed threshold reliably.
numpy memory pool recycles same-size allocations very efficiently: np.empty(3MB) then free is ~0 overhead. Pre-allocated buffers do not help for same-size allocations.
TruePeakProcessor.process bottleneck: 55.6% CPU at 48kHz. All of it is in cython _apply FIR convolution. No wrapper-level wins possible.
sosfilt bottleneck: 22.98% CPU. Input copy (40KB x 46 calls/round) = ~1.6% max improvement, below threshold.
np.sum(a*b) vs np.dot(a,b): for 2-element arrays, np.dot is 4.2x faster - MERGED PR #98
np.mean on 3-30 element slices: 4.8-5.1x slower than Python float running sum - MERGED PR #100
RunningSum property overhead: TESTED 2026-05-02 - NO improvement from replacing with plain float. Benchmark noise (~12% StdDev) masks any sub-1% wins.
TruePeakProcessor benchmark baselines (gate_size=1024, 2ch, local codspeed sim): 48kHz~62us/call, 88.2kHz~38us/call (2026-05-02 re-measured)
np.square(full_block) reuse (PR #95): +7% in momentary/short_term/lra per CodSpeed - MERGED
Fused square-sum (PR #114): ~2% improvement on momentary/short_term/lra (2026-05-03 MERGED).
einsum('ij,ij->i', x, x) vs np.square(x).sum(axis=1): einsum avoids intermediate allocation, 16-26% faster locally (2026-05-04). PR submitted.
CodSpeed simulation mode counts CPU instructions - Python dispatch overhead matters significantly
DO NOT use -p no:codspeed when running tests - it breaks benchmark tests (benchmark fixture missing). Use uv run pytest -m 'not slow' instead.
block_processor benchmark noise: StdDev ~13-16% on 2.2-3.0ms mean. Changes below ~0.5ms are undetectable.
AGENTS.md added 2026-05-02 - authoritative commands reference for all agents
checked_off_items: perf-assist/incremental-lra PR (checked off in monthly activity 2026-04)
PR #95 single-sq-pass review (checked off by maintainer in monthly activity 2026-04)

infrastructure_notes.codspeed_threshold: CodSpeed 'does not alter performance' = below 10% overall threshold. Individual benchmarks accessible via CodSpeed link.
infrastructure_notes.benchmark_coverage: All 4 block_processor modes: integrated, momentary, short_term, lra. Plus meter (4 sample rates). TruePeakProcessor standalone (48kHz 4x, 88.2kHz 2x). Filter benchmarks.
infrastructure_notes.what_shows_in_codspeed: Algorithmic changes (O(n^2) -> O(n)) or dispatch elimination for frequently-called operations. Sub-2% local improvements likely invisible in CodSpeed.
